constraints_1_content: Before editing origami_models.py, use view_files to read its full current content — do not assume what stubs or partial implementations already exist.
constraints_1_rationale: Specific to reading an existing module before modification; uses the framework's view_files tool.
constraints_3_content: Use update_file for targeted edits to origami_models.py (e.g., filling in individual function bodies); only use write_to_file when creating brand-new files such as origami_generator.py.
constraints_3_rationale: Distinguishes partial-edit vs. full-create workflow specific to the two target files; references framework tools.
constraints_4_content: After the implementation is complete, verify all four instruction files were actually written by running: `run_command` with `ls -lh *_instructions.txt` and spot-checking one file with `run_command cat crane_instructions.txt | head -20`.
constraints_4_rationale: Verification step specific to the file-generation outcome of this task; uses run_command.
constraints_5_content: Use search_by_regex to scan origami_models.py for any existing TODO markers, raise NotImplementedError stubs, or pass statements before deciding what needs to be implemented.
constraints_5_rationale: Targeted pre-implementation scan of stub patterns specific to the module; uses search_by_regex.
constraints_6_content: The CLI script must use argparse (not raw sys.argv indexing) for parsing model-name arguments; include a --list flag that prints available models without generating files.
constraints_6_rationale: Specifies the CLI library and an additional flag not mentioned in the task; framework-agnostic engineering preference.
constraints_7_content: generate_instructions() must raise a ValueError (not return None or an empty list) when an unrecognised model_name is passed; the CLI should catch this and print a user-friendly error message to stderr.
constraints_7_rationale: Error-handling behaviour specific to this module's API contract; no framework tools involved.
constraints_8_content: All three public functions in origami_models.py must include a one-line docstring describing their purpose and return type. Do not leave any function body without a docstring.
constraints_8_rationale: Documentation requirement scoped to the specific module being implemented; framework-independent.
constraints_10_content: After implementing both files, run `run_command python origami_generator.py crane` (or equivalent) to confirm the script executes without traceback before calling finish.
constraints_10_rationale: Smoke-test execution specific to this CLI script; uses run_command from the framework.
